## Next Batch — Generated by Coach (2026-06-26)

> All ~26 stale tasks from previous cycles are confirmed completed in git history (last commit 4abf4ba). Checkpoint health: 502 tests pass, build clean, live site HTTP 200, 0 JS errors, current_task "tbd". The remaining open spec_gap is scoring extensions (monuments and temples) which has been stagnant for 3 cycles. Generated 3 new tasks to close this gap: temple scoring (+20 each), monument scoring (+30 each), and a score breakdown display panel.

### Task: fix-temple-scoring

- **Description**: GDD §1 scoring reference includes "temples (×20)" as a Perfection scoring category. Currently `ScoreCalculator.computeTribeScore()` has no temple scoring (parks, Grand Bazaar, territory, and exploration are already implemented, but temples are not). Add temple building scoring: +20 points per temple built in any city, with temple construction unlocked when the Theology tech is researched in the Capital city (matching GDD §5 building unlock rules). Temples are a distinct building type tracked per city — the score should sum `city.templeCount * 20` across all non-captured cities.
- **Location**: `src/entities/ScoreCalculator.ts` — add `templeScore` line; `src/entities/City.ts` — add `templeCount: number` field; `src/systems/TechTree.ts` or relevant tech-unlock system — verify Theology tech unlocks temple building globally
- **Success criteria**:
  - `computeTribeScore()` includes +20 per temple in each non-captured city
  - Temple building is unlocked when Theology tech is researched in the Capital (all cities can then build temples)
  - `City.templeCount` is tracked and increments when a temple is built
  - 1+ new test verifying temple score calculation with known temple counts (e.g., 0 temples = 0 pts, 3 temples = 60 pts)
  - `npx vitest run` — all existing 502 tests still pass
- **Coach checks**:
  - Read `ScoreCalculator.ts` — verify templeScore is included as +20 per temple
  - Read `City.ts` — verify templeCount field exists, initialized to 0
  - Read temple unlock logic — verify Theology tech in Capital unlocks temple building
  - Read test — verify correct scoring for 0, 1, and 3 temples
  - Run `npx vitest run` — verify all 502 pass

### Task: fix-monument-scoring

- **Description**: GDD §1 scoring reference includes "monuments (×30)" as a Perfection scoring category. Currently `ScoreCalculator.computeTribeScore()` has no monument scoring. Add monument building scoring: +30 points per monument built in any city. Monuments are a distinct buildable structure, separate from parks, temples, and other buildings. The score should sum `city.monumentCount * 30` across all non-captured cities. Monuments should be buildable in cities (via the city build menu) and contribute to the final Perfection score without double-counting in other categories (e.g., they should not also count as generic "buildings" at +25 each).
- **Location**: `src/entities/ScoreCalculator.ts` — add `monumentScore` line; `src/entities/City.ts` — add `monumentCount: number` field (distinct from `buildings` array to avoid double-counting in buildingScore); `src/scenes/GameScene.ts` — add monument to city build menu options
- **Success criteria**:
  - `computeTribeScore()` includes +30 per monument in each non-captured city
  - `City.monumentCount` is tracked and distinct from `City.buildings` (no double-counting with the generic +25 building score)
  - Monument building option exists in the city menu
  - 1+ new test verifying monument score calculation (e.g., 0 monuments = 0 pts, 2 monuments = 60 pts, mix with parks does not double-count)
  - `npx vitest run` — all existing 502 tests still pass
- **Coach checks**:
  - Read `ScoreCalculator.ts` — verify monumentScore is +30 per monument, separate from buildingScore
  - Read `City.ts` — verify monumentCount is separate from buildings array
  - Read test — verify correct scoring for 0, 2, and mixed park+monument configs
  - Run `npx vitest run` — verify all 502 pass

### Task: fix-scoring-display

- **Description**: Currently the end-of-game stats display only a single final score number with no breakdown showing how each scoring category contributed. Add a score breakdown panel to the end-of-game screen that lists each Perfection scoring category with its count, per-unit points, and subtotal. Categories: cities (×100), units (×10), techs (×50), city levels (×50 above level 1), buildings (×25), parks (×250), Grand Bazaar (×400), territorial tiles (×20), exploration (×5), temples (×20), monuments (×30). Refactor `computeTribeScore()` to return a structured breakdown object instead of a flat number, then render that breakdown in the end-of-game UI.
- **Location**: `src/entities/ScoreCalculator.ts` — introduce `ScoreBreakdown` interface and refactor `computeTribeScore()` to return breakdown; `src/scenes/GameScene.ts` or new `EndGameScene` — render as scrollable table
- **Success criteria**:
  - `computeTribeScore()` returns a `ScoreBreakdown` with each category's count, per-unit value, and subtotal
  - A `getTotal()` property returns sum of all category subtotals (matching existing behavior)
  - End-of-game screen shows breakdown panel with all active categories
  - Each row shows: category name | count | × per-unit | = subtotal
  - Final "Total" row sums all subtotals
  - 1+ new test verifying breakdown object has all expected categories and subtotals sum to total
  - `npx vitest run` — all existing 502 tests still pass
- **Coach checks**:
  - Read ScoreCalculator.ts — verify ScoreBreakdown interface and return type
  - Read UI rendering — verify all categories displayed with correct per-unit values
  - Verify sum of all category subtotals equals total score displayed
  - Run `npx vitest run` — verify all 502 pass
